name: senior-devops
description: Comprehensive DevOps skill for CI/CD, infrastructure automation, containerization, and cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure). Includes pipeline setup, infrastructure as code, deployment automation, and monitoring. Use when setting up pipelines, deploying applications, managing infrastructure, implementing monitoring, or optimizing deployment processes.
capabilities: Generate CI/CD pipeline configurations for GitHub Actions, CircleCI, and other platforms
Create Terraform and infrastructure as code templates for multi-cloud deployments
Implement deployment strategies including blue-green, canary, and rolling deployments
Provide guidance on Docker containerization and Kubernetes orchestration patterns
Analyze existing infrastructure and recommend optimization strategies
Generate reference documentation for DevOps patterns and best practices
author: davila7
securitySummary: Three Python scaffolding scripts that generate configurations for CI/CD, Terraform, and deployment workflows. No network access, no shell execution, no credential access. Filesystem writes only to user-specified paths for report output. All 72 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation keywords and code block syntax misinterpreted as shell commands.
valueStatement: Setting up CI/CD pipelines and managing cloud infrastructure requires deep expertise across multiple platforms. This skill provides templates, patterns, and automation scripts for Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, and major cloud providers.
userTitle: Automate DevOps with CI/CD and IaC expertise
limitations: Scripts generate templates and configurations but do not execute deployments
Does not provision actual cloud resources or manage live environments
Requires user to review and apply generated configurations manually
Scripts are scaffolding tools that output templates for user customization

useCases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Setup
useCases.description: Generate production-ready CI/CD pipelines with testing, security scanning, and deployment gates

useCases.title: Infrastructure Templates
useCases.description: Create Terraform modules and IaC patterns for scalable cloud infrastructure on AWS, Azure, and GCP

useCases.title: Container Orchestration
useCases.description: Design Kubernetes deployments, Helm charts, and container strategies for microservices

promptTemplates.title: Basic Pipeline Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a CI/CD pipeline for my [language] project on [platform]. Include stages for build, test, security scan, and deploy to [environment].

promptTemplates.title: Infrastructure Code
promptTemplates.prompt: Create Terraform configuration for [aws/azure/gcp] infrastructure. Include [vpc/networking], [ec2/compute], and [rds/database] resources with proper tagging.

promptTemplates.title: Deployment Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a [blue-green/canary/rolling] deployment strategy for Kubernetes. Include service configuration, readiness probes, and rollback procedures.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Cloud Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create infrastructure as code for a multi-cloud architecture with [aws and gcp]. Include VPC peering, load balancing, and secret management configuration.

bestPractices: Provide specific details about your tech stack, cloud provider, and deployment target for accurate configurations
Review generated Terraform and pipeline code before applying in production environments
Use the reference guides in the skill to understand the patterns before customization
antiPatterns: Asking the skill to directly execute Terraform or deploy to live cloud environments
Expecting production-ready credentials or secrets management in generated templates
Requesting complex multi-cloud configurations without specifying integration requirements

faq.question: Does this skill execute Terraform or deploy resources?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates configuration files and templates that you review and apply manually.

faq.question: What CI/CD platforms are supported?
faq.answer: GitHub Actions, CircleCI, GitLab CI, and Jenkins with platform-specific templates.

faq.question: Which cloud providers work with this skill?
faq.answer: AWS, Azure, and GCP with Terraform configurations for common infrastructure patterns.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using these scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. Scripts only access user-specified paths and make no network calls or credential access.

faq.question: Why did I get a generic template?
faq.answer: Provide more details about your stack, cloud provider, and requirements for specific configurations.

faq.question: How is this different from infrastructure consulting?
faq.answer: This skill provides code and configurations. Complex architectural decisions still require human expertise.
